tossups.question: A 1972 Cuban film by Humberto Solás lightly orchestrates a simple A minor guitar lament titled for “a day in” this time period by Leo Brouwer. Early into an affair with pianist Harriet Cohen, Arnold Bax wrote a turbulent but anticlimactic tone poem titled for this period. A Nikolay Nekrasov poem warns “don’t rush after the troika” in the epigraph of a moderato E major short movement for solo piano titled for this period, which opens with uneven C-sharp, B, long E in unison and is interrupted by a folksy grazioso imitating sleigh bells. Eclipse preceded a concertante piece that juxtaposes Western orchestra with biwa and shakuhachi that Takemitsu titled for this period’s “steps.” For 10 points, the penultimate movement of Tchaikovsky’s The Seasons is titled for what month?
tossups.question_sanitized: A 1972 Cuban film by Humberto Solas lightly orchestrates a simple A minor guitar lament titled for "a day in" this time period by Leo Brouwer. Early into an affair with pianist Harriet Cohen, Arnold Bax wrote a turbulent but anticlimactic tone poem titled for this period. A Nikolay Nekrasov poem warns "don't rush after the troika" in the epigraph of a moderato E major short movement for solo piano titled for this period, which opens with uneven C-sharp, B, long E in unison and is interrupted by a folksy grazioso imitating sleigh bells. Eclipse preceded a concertante piece that juxtaposes Western orchestra with biwa and shakuhachi that Takemitsu titled for this period's "steps." For 10 points, the penultimate movement of Tchaikovsky's The Seasons is titled for what month?
tossups.answer_sanitized: November [Un dia de noviembre, November Woods, November Steps]

tossups.question: John Williams believed that a folk instrument from this country sounded “like a dinosaur’s cry,” which led him to include it in the score for Jurassic Park. The “1.8” variety of that instrument from this country has a fundamental of D4 and is tuned to the minor pentatonic scale. Musical instruments from this country were featured in a piece the New York Philharmonic commissioned for its 125th anniversary, November Steps. A traditional flute from this country is used in the practice of “blowing meditation” practiced by the fuke sect of monks. This country’s national instrument, a typically 13-stringed zither called a koto, is often included in gagaku orchestras. For 10 points, name this country whose traditional instruments include the taiko drum and shakuhachi flute.
tossups.answer: Japan [or Nihon-koku or Nippon-koku] (November Steps is by Tōru Takemitsu.)

packet.name: M - Liberty B, Northwestern A, NYU A, Warwick A, Waterloo A

tossups.answer_sanitized: Japan [or Nihon-koku or Nippon-koku] (November Steps is by Toru Takemitsu.)

bonuses.leadin: Zen Buddhism likely inspired this composer to create a set of “Sonic Meditations,” one of which asks participants to “take a walk at night.” For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: Zen Buddhism likely inspired this composer to create a set of "Sonic Meditations," one of which asks participants to "take a walk at night." For 10 points each:
parts: Name this American composer, who coined the term “deep listening” after going underground to record a composition. This composer co-founded the San Francisco Tape Music Center with Robert Erickson.

John Cage, who was inspired by Zen Buddhism to compose 4’33’’, studied under a Buddhist scholar with this surname. Violin students often learn via a “method” named for a Japanese musician with this surname.

Cage in turn influenced Toru Takemitsu, who embraced Buddhist aesthetics in this piece for shakuhachi, biwa, and orchestra. On the advice of Jasper Johns, Takemitsu changed this piece’s title from “Water Rings” to one that reflects when it was premiered.
parts_sanitized: Name this American composer, who coined the term "deep listening" after going underground to record a composition. This composer co-founded the San Francisco Tape Music Center with Robert Erickson.

John Cage, who was inspired by Zen Buddhism to compose 4'33'', studied under a Buddhist scholar with this surname. Violin students often learn via a "method" named for a Japanese musician with this surname.

Cage in turn influenced Toru Takemitsu, who embraced Buddhist aesthetics in this piece for shakuhachi, biwa, and orchestra. On the advice of Jasper Johns, Takemitsu changed this piece's title from "Water Rings" to one that reflects when it was premiered.
answers: Suzuki [accept D. T. Suzuki or Daisetz Teitaro Suzuki or Shinichi Suzuki]
answers_sanitized: Suzuki [accept D. T. Suzuki or Daisetz Teitaro Suzuki or Shinichi Suzuki]
